name: Dynamic Customer Group created (session-attribute rule)
description: SFCC OCAPI Data API response to PUT .../customer_groups/CG-SFloyalty-20OFFLEGO. The Middleware creates one dynamic group per unique promotion; its rule matches when the shopper's session.custom.loyaltyPromotions CONTAINS the code. This is the mechanism that activates the static PMID per-session.
notes: OPERATOR IS L/DASH: the operator MUST be `contains` (a SUBSTRING test) with a single string value — session.custom.loyaltyPromotions is a comma-joined string (e.g. 'SFloyalty-20OFFLEGO,SFloyalty-FreeShipping'), so a set-membership/equality operator (is-one-of) would evaluate FALSE for any stacked member and the group would never activate. The design doc's rule ({operator:'CONTAINS', attribute_name, value}) is the intent; OCAPI serialises it as conditions[] with attribute_path + operator + value under a customer_group_rule (design intent in request.body, OCAPI shape in response). OCAPI operators are lower-case tokens with no spaces. 200 = update/idempotent (PUT); first create can return 201; _resource_state is the ETag.
